one_line_thesis: Abacus Global Management is a life-settlement and alternative-asset platform listed on the NYSE.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Abacus Global Management is a life-settlement and alternative-asset platform listed on the NYSE. The company originates and services life insurance policies, runs asset-management sleeves (including FCF Advisors, acquired December 2024), and sells mortality-verification technology through Abacus Intel. FY2025 revenue reached $235 million, up 110% year over year, while adjusted EBITDA was $133 million. GAAP cash from operations was only $26 million because policy purchases and working capital absorb cash before mortality payoffs arrive.

At roughly $10.26 per share, the stock trades near 2.2...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_provenance.label: Owner earnings and reinvestment-return DCF
method_provenance.equation: cash_t = cash_(t-1)*(1+reinvestment*incremental_ROIC); value = PV(distributable cash + terminal owner earnings)

sources.locator: Owner cash, reinvestment and reverse expectations

low.label: Normalized owner earnings per diluted share
low.rationale: FY2025 operating income per share anchors Life Solutions and servicing economics; policy portfolio fair-value marks flow through earnings, not a separate additive NAV layer.

low.label: Duration-adjusted reinvestment capitalization multiple
low.rationale: Bear stresses longevity risk and funding spread widening; base mid-cycle rollup path; bull modest securitization and origination scale without peak-cycle heroics.

base.rationale: FY2025 operating income per share anchors Life Solutions and servicing economics; policy portfolio fair-value marks flow through earnings, not a separate additive NAV layer.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Seven-year horizon aligned with Lawrence model; commercial traction expected years 3–5 if Abacus Intel gains institutional verification clients.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Base case assumes 15–25% probability of scaled third-party API adoption within seven years given $0.7M FY2025 Technology Services revenue.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Incremental R&D and sales capital for Abacus Intel embedded in consolidated operating spend; no separate owner-funded capex line disclosed.

components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low case zero; base/high bound API monetization beyond Life Solutions servicing.
components.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

options.evidence: Primary bridge from ABX/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260313_rpt20251231_acc0001628280_26_017775.htm: FY2025 revenue $235.238M, operating income $88.757M, OCF $25.680465M; ABX/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260511_rpt20260331_acc0001628280_26_033136.htm: Q1 cash $37.21M, debt $291.844M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Seven-year horizon aligned with Lawrence model; commercial traction expected years 3–5 if Abacus Intel gains institutional verification clients.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Base case assumes 15–25% probability of scaled third-party API adoption within seven years given $0.7M FY2025 Technology Services revenue.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Incremental R&D and sales capital for Abacus Intel embedded in consolidated operating spend; no separate owner-funded capex line disclosed.

options.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low case zero; base/high bound API monetization beyond Life Solutions servicing.
options.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q filing-derived inputs. Adjusted EBITDA is narrative context only; Lawrence base uses GAAP owner cash.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of ABX, including every identified Life Solutions operating claim, asset-management fee franchise, Abacus Intel technology option, net financial claims, and longevity/funding reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and option claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components with non-overlapping overlap keys.

timeline.label: Q1 2026 results: revenue $59.4M, net income $7.3M, operating cash flow $91.7M (lumpy policy timing).
2026 annual meeting: director elections and LTIP approval.
FY2025 Form 10-K: revenue $235.2M (+110% YoY), adjusted EBITDA $132.6M, long-term debt $405.8M.
Declared $0.20 annual cash dividend; shelf registration filed.
Closed $50M investment-grade securitized note to fund Life Solutions growth.
Renamed from Abacus Life, Inc. to Abacus Global Management, Inc.
Acquired FCF Advisors, expanding asset-management franchise.
Launched Abacus Intel mortality-verification technology (Technology Services segment).
Carlisle acquisition doubled origination scale; shares diluted to ~99M.
NYSE listing via SPAC combination; dual equity and ABXL note structure established.

industry.structure: Life-settlement origination and servicing is fragmented; scale players combine underwriting data, warehouse funding, and institutional buyer networks. Barriers include actuarial expertise, regulatory licensing, and securitization access.
industry.share_shift: Abacus gained share through Carlisle and FCF Advisors roll-ups; revenue doubled FY2024–FY2025. Smaller originators remain regional competitors.
industry.trend: Institutional demand for life-policy assets persists; securitized funding lowers cost of capital for scaled platforms. Longevity risk and credit spreads remain key swing factors.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Mortality/longevity assumptions prove too optimistic on acquired policies, compressing spreads while debt service remains fixed.
Funding market disruption or higher spreads on warehouse and ABXL notes
Integration failure across Carlisle, FCF Advisors, and legacy Abacus cultures
